name: ingress
context: Route external HTTP/HTTPS traffic into a Kubernetes cluster with Ingress resources and controllers
info: Ingress exposes HTTP and HTTPS routes from outside the cluster to [[kubernetes/services]] inside the cluster. An Ingress resource defines routing rules (host, path, TLS), and an Ingress Controller (nginx, Traefik, AWS ALB) implements them. Agents use Ingress to route traffic by hostname and path, terminate TLS with certificates, and consolidate multiple services behind a single load balancer. This skill covers Ingress manifests, path-based and host-based routing, TLS termination, annotations for controller-specific features, and IngressClass selection.
triggers: route traffic to a kubernetes service
add a hostname to kubernetes ingress
body:
# Ingress

Ingress manages external HTTP/HTTPS access to [[kubernetes/services]]. It provides URL-based routing, TLS termination, and virtual hosting — all with a single external load balancer.

## How It Works

```
Client → LoadBalancer → Ingress Controller (Pod) → Service → Pods
                         (nginx, traefik, ALB)
```

Two pieces required:
1. **Ingress Controller** — a Pod running a reverse proxy (nginx, Traefik, etc.) that watches Ingress resources and configures routing
2. **Ingress Resource** — a YAML manifest defining routing rules

## Install Ingress Controller

```bash
# nginx ingress controller (most common)
helm repo add ingress-nginx https://kubernetes.github.io/ingress-nginx
helm repo update
helm install ingress-nginx ingress-nginx/ingress-nginx \
  --namespace ingress-nginx \
  --create-namespace

# Verify
kubectl get pods -n ingress-nginx
kubectl get svc -n ingress-nginx  # EXTERNAL-IP shows the LB address

# Traefik (alternative)
helm repo add traefik https://traefik.github.io/charts
helm install traefik traefik/traefik --namespace traefik --create-namespace
```

## Basic Ingress (single service)

```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: web-app
  namespace: production
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
    - host: app.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: web-app
                port:
                  number: 80
```

## Host-Based Routing (virtual hosting)

```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: multi-host
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
    - host: app.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: frontend
                port:
                  number: 80
    - host: api.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: api-service
                port:
                  number: 8080
```

## Path-Based Routing

```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: path-routing
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
    - host: app.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /api
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: api-service
                port:
                  number: 8080
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: frontend
                port:
                  number: 80
```

Path types: `Prefix` (matches `/api` and `/api/users`), `Exact` (matches only `/api`), `ImplementationSpecific` (controller-dependent).

## TLS Termination

```yaml
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: tls-ingress
  annotations:
    cert-manager.io/cluster-issuer: letsencrypt-prod  # auto-provision certs
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  tls:
    - hosts:
        - app.example.com
        - api.example.com
      secretName: example-com-tls  # Secret with tls.crt and tls.key
  rules:
    - host: app.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: web-app
                port:
                  number: 80
```

The TLS Secret must contain `tls.crt` and `tls.key`. Create manually or auto-provision with cert-manager:

```bash
# Manual TLS secret
kubectl create secret tls example-com-tls \
  --cert=./tls.crt \
  --key=./tls.key

# Or use cert-manager for automatic Let's Encrypt certificates
helm repo add jetstack https://charts.jetstack.io
helm install cert-manager jetstack/cert-manager \
  --namespace cert-manager \
  --create-namespace \
  --set crds.enabled=true
```

## Common Annotations (nginx)

```yaml
annotations:
  nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /$1         # URL rewriting
  nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: "true"        # Force HTTPS
  nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-body-size: "50m"      # Max upload size
  nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/proxy-read-timeout: "300"   # Backend timeout
  nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/cors-allow-origin: "*"      # CORS
  nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rate-limit-rps: "10"        # Rate limiting
  nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/auth-url: "https://auth.example.com/verify"  # External auth
```

## kubectl Commands

```bash
# List ingresses
kubectl get ingress -n production
kubectl get ing -A  # all namespaces

# Describe (shows rules, backends, events)
kubectl describe ingress web-app

# Check ingress controller logs
kubectl logs -n ingress-nginx -l app.kubernetes.io/name=ingress-nginx --tail=50

# Delete
kubectl delete ingress web-app
```

## Ingress Controllers Comparison

| Controller | Strengths | Install |
|------------|-----------|----------|
| nginx-ingress | Most popular, widely supported, rich annotations | Helm chart |
| Traefik | Auto-discovery, middleware chains, dashboard | Helm chart |
| AWS ALB | Native AWS integration, WAF, target groups | AWS Load Balancer Controller |
| GCE | Native GCP integration, Cloud CDN, Cloud Armor | Built into GKE |
| Contour | Envoy-based, HTTPProxy CRD, advanced routing | Helm chart |

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Purpose |
|----------|----------|
| `KUBECONFIG` | Path to kubeconfig file |
| `KUBE_NAMESPACE` | Default namespace |

sections.title: Routing rules
sections.body: Ingress routes by host and path. Host-based routing sends different hostnames to different [[kubernetes/services]] (virtual hosting). Path-based routing sends different URL paths to different services. Path types: Prefix (matches path and sub-paths), Exact (exact match only), ImplementationSpecific. Rules are evaluated in order — more specific paths should come first. A default backend handles unmatched requests.

sections.title: TLS termination
sections.body: Ingress terminates TLS at the controller. Specify a Secret containing tls.crt and tls.key in `spec.tls`. Create Secrets manually with `kubectl create secret tls` or automatically with cert-manager (Let's Encrypt). The controller handles the TLS handshake and forwards decrypted traffic to backend [[kubernetes/services]]. Use `nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/ssl-redirect: true` to force HTTPS.

sections.title: Ingress controllers
sections.body: An Ingress resource does nothing without a controller. Install one: nginx-ingress (most common, Helm chart), Traefik (auto-discovery, middleware), AWS ALB Controller (native AWS), GCE (native GCP). Set `spec.ingressClassName` to select which controller handles the Ingress. Multiple controllers can coexist in the same cluster for different use cases.

links.context: Ingress routes to Services as backends
TLS Secrets store certificates for Ingress

related.note: Ingress routes traffic to Services — Services must exist first
Ingress controllers are typically installed via Helm charts
TLS Secrets used by Ingress are managed the same way as other Secrets
